Skip to main content

Start with the problem. We’ll find the right form.

CONTACT

Start with the problem, not a polished brief.

Electronic Artefacts welcomes product, platform, system-recovery, technology-partnership and cultural-project conversations. A useful first message explains what needs to change, why it matters now and which constraints or unknowns already shape the situation.

Product and platform work.

Bring a new proposition, an existing service that needs clearer structure, or a platform whose interface and architecture have drifted apart. Context about users, ownership, deadlines and current technology helps identify the right start.

System investigation and recovery.

For compromised, undocumented or fragile systems, describe the observable symptoms, available backups and operational urgency. No untrusted files need to be sent before a safe review path has been agreed.

Technology, research and cultural partnerships.

VASTE pilots, repository reviews, research collaborations, publications and artistic projects can begin with a focused question. The studio will clarify the relevant evidence and form of collaboration.

Direct contact remains simple.

Email is the primary route for a considered introduction. The interactive brief on this page works locally in the browser and can prepare a structured message without uploading project information to the website.

Send an emailReview selected workUnderstand the studio
